title: The Wright's Chaste Wife
summary: The General Corpus XML is a repository copy of University of Michigan CME item CME00054. The U-M encoding reproduces the title page and complete 672-line poem from Frederick J. Furnivall's 1869 revised second edition of EETS Original Series 12, while omitting the edition's preface, notes, glossary, companion poem, and other matter. That revised edition descends from Furnivall's 1865 first edition, which he transcribed directly from the unique witness, Lambeth Palace Library MS 306, fols. 178r-187r. A complete 415-image color IIIF bundle of MS 306 is cached privately at a practical access resolution; the work is mapped inside the full codex at canvases 359-377 rather than cropped out. DIMEV v1.0 and Eve Salisbury's 2002 METS edition are collateral modern sources. Furnivall's claim that Keller's Gesta Romanorum chapter 69 was the poem's immediate original remains explicitly unresolved.
review_notes: Lambeth's IIIF canvas labels are generic numbers 1-415. The folio mapping was established from the full sequence and verified visually against front and rear physical matter, poem boundaries, the catalog locus, and the regular foliation sequence.

The cached MS 306 bundle includes every provider canvas: front cover, inside front board and pastedown, preliminary leaf, all folios, inside rear board and pastedown with fragment, rear cover, and spine. No separate top, fore, or bottom edge views are exposed.
Furnivall's title page says '[Second Edition, revised, 1869]' while retaining an MDCCCLXV imprint. DIMEV independently distinguishes the 1865 first edition from the 1869 revised second edition.
The U-M browse label '(1875)' conflicts with the inspected title page and DIMEV and is recorded as a catalog-layer error, not an edition date.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00054
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's publication workflow and supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify CME00054.
TEMPHEAD records a final review on 2016-09-19.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00054
entities.description: Public Middle English text encoding Furnivall's revised-edition title page and complete poem, including editorial marginal summaries, six footnotes, printed page breaks, manuscript leaf milestones, and italic expansion/supply markup.

entities.label: Furnivall's 1869 revised second edition of The Wright's Chaste Wife
entities.description: The immediate print source of CME00054: the revised second edition of EETS Original Series 12, explicitly dated 1869 in brackets while retaining the first edition's 1865 imprint.

bibliographic.citation: Adam of Cobsam, The Wright's Chaste Wife, copied and edited by Frederick J. Furnivall, 2nd ed., revised 1869, EETS Original Series 12 (London: N. Trübner & Co. for the Early English Text Society, MDCCCLXV [1865]).
bibliographic.title: The Wright's Chaste Wife
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 12
bibliographic.publisher: N. Trübner & Co. for the Early English Text Society
bibliographic.pages: viii, 26 printed pages in the scanned issue

notes: The poem occupies printed pp. 1-19. Preface, P.P.S. 1869, notes, glossary, and the companion poem 'Women' are outside CME00054's encoded selection.

entities.label: Furnivall's 1865 first edition of The Wright's Chaste Wife
entities.description: The first edition of EETS Original Series 12, based on Furnivall's direct transcription of Lambeth MS 306 and revised for the immediate 1869 second edition.

bibliographic.citation: Adam of Cobsam, The Wright's Chaste Wife, copied and edited by Frederick J. Furnivall, EETS Original Series 12 (London: N. Trübner & Co. for the Early English Text Society, 1865).
bibliographic.title: The Wright's Chaste Wife
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 12
bibliographic.publisher: N. Trübner & Co. for the Early English Text Society

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library MS 306
entities.description: The unique manuscript witness of The Wright's Chaste Wife. The poem occupies Booklet 10, ff. 178r-187r, copied by Scribe H; the current codex is a composite of ten or eleven independent booklets bound together c. 1504-1512.

date_statements.label: Work witness dating
date_statements.value: 1460s in the modern METS bibliography; Scribe H conservatively dated to the fifteenth century by Lambeth
date_statements.notes: The normalized range represents the METS witness dating, not a firm composition range for the poem.

holding.notes: Item 26; Booklet 10; later owned and annotated elsewhere in the codex by John Stow.

physical_description.extent: ff. i + 204; The Wright's Chaste Wife on ff. 178r-187r
physical_description.format: Composite codex of ten or eleven independent booklets
notes: Blind-stamped leather over wooden boards, rebacked; both clasps missing.
Scribe H writes a fifteenth-century Secretary hand with Anglicana features, approximately 37 lines per page.
The poem begins below a medical recipe on f. 178r and ends above further medical recipes on f. 187r; these shared physical surfaces remain in the full facsimile mapping.

entities.label: Lambeth complete public digital sequence for MS 306
entities.description: The complete 415-canvas color sequence exposed by Lambeth, including the front cover, inside front board and pastedown, preliminary leaf, ff. 1r-204v, inside rear board and pastedown with fragment, rear cover, and spine. The poem occupies canvases 359-377 / provider page indices 358-376.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and 415 color JPEG access derivatives

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Furnivall's 1869 revised second edition
entities.description: A 41-page Harvard Library volume scan digitized by Google and made available through Internet Archive, including Google access matter and the complete scanned Furnivall volume.
entities.reproduction_format: Google Books PDF scan distributed by Internet Archive

entities.label: W. A. Clouston's 1886 Additional Analogs to The Wright's Chaste Wife
entities.description: A separately published EETS supplement collecting comparative analogues. It is bound after Furnivall's revised second edition in Internet Archive item wrightschastewi00furngoog and is a later supporting source, not an ancestor of CME00054.

bibliographic.citation: W. A. Clouston, Supplement to The Wright's Chaste Wife: Additional Analogs to The Wright's Chaste Wife, EETS Original Series 84 (London: N. Trübner & Co. for the Early English Text Society, 1886), pp. 25-39.
bibliographic.title: Supplement to The Wright's Chaste Wife: Additional Analogs to The Wright's Chaste Wife
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 84
bibliographic.publisher: N. Trübner & Co. for the Early English Text Society

entities.label: Internet Archive complete bound scan of Furnivall 1869 with Clouston 1886 supplement
entities.description: A 112-page Google scan of a bound volume containing Furnivall's 1869 revised second edition, Clouston's 1886 Additional Analogs supplement, and further bound publisher or reference matter. Internet Archive metadata dates the item 1865, but the inspected internal title page explicitly says revised second edition 1869 and the supplement is dated 1886.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete 112-page Google Books PDF scan distributed by Internet Archive

entities.label: Digital Index of Middle English Verse version 1.0
entities.description: Versioned CC BY 4.0 data identifying DIMEV/NIMEV 252, the unique Lambeth witness, manuscript title and author attribution, verse form, and Furnivall edition sequence.

entities.label: Eve Salisbury's 2002 METS edition of The Wright's Chaste Wife
entities.description: A later collateral scholarly edition in The Trials and Joys of Marriage, with glossed text, select bibliography, textual and explanatory notes, and contextual classification. It is not an ancestor of CME00054.

bibliographic.citation: Eve Salisbury, ed., The Trials and Joys of Marriage, Middle English Texts Series (Kalamazoo, MI: Medieval Institute Publications, 2002), The Wright's Chaste Wife.
bibliographic.title: The Trials and Joys of Marriage

entities.label: METS downloadable PDF of Salisbury's The Trials and Joys of Marriage
entities.description: The complete 277-page downloadable PDF currently linked from the METS edition page; The Wright's Chaste Wife text is at PDF pp. 62-79, followed by its bibliography and notes.

entities.label: Adelbert von Keller's 1842 edition of Gesta Romanorum, volume 1
entities.description: Keller's Latin edition containing chapter 69, De castitate, printed pp. 107-109. Furnivall's 1869 postscript identifies this chapter as the immediate original of The Wright's Chaste Wife; that source claim is not treated as settled.

bibliographic.citation: Gesta Romanorum, ed. Adelbert von Keller, vol. 1, Text (Stuttgart and Tübingen: J. G. Cotta, 1842).
bibliographic.title: Gesta Romanorum

entities.label: Wikimedia Commons complete scan of Keller's Gesta Romanorum (1842)
entities.description: A 320-page complete PDF scan of Keller's volume, derived from the Internet Archive item dli.granth.37769 and marked Public Domain on Wikimedia Commons.

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library reprographic and publication-permission service
entities.description: The official order and permission route for high-quality manuscript images, research PDFs, and permission to reproduce Lambeth images. It is an access service, not a textual ancestor.

agents.name: Adam of Cobsam
notes: Traditionally treated as author because he is named at line 620; modern METS marks authorship uncertain.

agents.name: Scribe H
notes: Unidentified fifteenth-century scribe of MS 306, ff. 178-187r; the initials on f. 178r are too early to be John Stow according to the Lambeth catalog.

agents.name: Digital Index of Middle English Verse

scope.notes: Matching exact identifier, title page, text, source structure, and CME repository provenance support the relation; exact transfer date is not recorded.

The title page explicitly calls the 1869 issue a revised second edition; DIMEV distinguishes the edition sequence.

object.description: The Trials and Joys of Marriage

scope.notes: Furnivall reports C. H. Pearson's identification and calls the chapter the immediate original. The edge records the historical citation, not a confirmed direct-adaptation chain. Keller's chapter is an analogue/source claim with zero target-text overlap, so its locator is scoped to this relation rather than modeled as a work portion of CME00054.

Lambeth LUNA compound-book viewer and IIIF Presentation manifest
access.format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and 415 color JPEG images

local_copies.label: Lambeth Palace Library MS 306 complete public IIIF sequence

work_portion.label: The Wright's Chaste Wife within the complete MS 306 facsimile
notes: Canvas 359 / f. 178r contains a preceding medical recipe above the poem opening; canvas 377 / f. 187r contains the poem ending and following medical material on the same leaf.
The complete bundle retains all adjacent works, blanks, binding matter, and physical surfaces exposed by the provider.
ZIP integrity passed; inventory, source, canvas, and image counts are all 415.

The provider labels canvases only with generic numbers 1-415. Visual and sequence audit maps canvas 1 to front cover, 2 to inside front board/pastedown, 3-4 to fol. i recto/verso, 5 to fol. 1r, 411-412 to fol. 204r/v, 413 to inside rear board/pastedown and fragment, 414 to rear cover, and 415 to spine.

Requested IIIF profile was width-only '1800,' for 414 canvases. The narrow spine did not expose that derivative and was retained at native 'full' size; the inventory records every exact successful request URL and effective profile.

Sampled ordinary images decode at 1800 x 2700 pixels; the rear cover is 1800 x 2699 and the native spine is 1249 x 5559. The files are practical color research derivatives, not asserted preservation masters.
Coverage is complete for the provider-published physical manuscript sequence. No separate top, fore, or bottom edge photographs are exposed.
Public viewing and technical download do not establish permission to redistribute Lambeth photographs. The ignored local bundle is for private research pending permission.

access.access_method: Complete and return the digital-photography form; submit the separate permission form for publication or repository reproduction
access.format: High-quality TIFF/JPEG or standard PDF by request; separate use-specific permission

local_copies.label: Lambeth Palace Library digital photography order form
notes: Complete official form and conditions, retained as a supporting access source rather than manuscript content.

local_copies.label: Lambeth Palace Library permission to reproduce form
notes: Complete official form and use conditions, retained as a supporting rights source rather than manuscript content.

The form lists high-quality TIFF/JPEG at £14.40 each including VAT (£12 excluding VAT) and standard research PDFs at £8.40 for the first item and £1.20 for subsequent images including VAT; payment is quoted before work begins.
The published form limits ordinary orders to 40 scans and allows refusal, so a whole 415-image preservation-resolution order or missing edge views require advance staff discussion.
The standard PDF is stated to be for basic research and unsuitable for reproduction. High-quality images and publication permission are separate requests.

access.format: Microfilm; World Microfilms reel also contains MSS 298 and 328
notes: The catalog proves the reproduction route but does not state whether the film includes all bindings and leaves; coverage remains unknown until inspected or confirmed by Lambeth.

rights.component: The fifteenth-century textual content of The Wright's Chaste Wife on ff. 178r-187r
rights.basis: The underlying Middle English work and unique manuscript copy predate modern copyright by centuries; U-M also labels its public-domain edition collection No Copyright-United States.
notes: This does not determine rights or contractual conditions in modern photographs, editorial contributions, encodings, or supplied reproductions.

rights.component: Materials presented in the U-M Corpus of Middle English, including item CME00054
rights.basis: The current U-M collection page says the materials are in the public domain in the United States and applies No Copyright-United States.
notes: The statement is jurisdiction-specific and does not govern Lambeth photographs or the modern METS edition.

rights.component: Furnivall's 1869 revised second edition, including its editorial contribution
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1869; the exact Google scan front matter and U-M collection both identify the historical book/material as public domain in the United States.

rights.component: Google/Harvard scan files and Google access front matter
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The PDF says the scanned book entered the public domain, but Google's embedded usage guidelines request personal, noncommercial use, no automated querying, retained attribution, and lawful territorial use.
notes: The book's public-domain status and the provider's requested file-use conditions are separate layers. The cached PDF remains Git-ignored.

rights.component: Google scan of the bound Furnivall 1869 edition, Clouston 1886 supplement, and associated bound matter
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: Internet Archive marks the item NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT in the United States, and the embedded Google front matter treats the historical book as public domain while requesting personal, noncommercial use, no automated querying, retained attribution, and lawful territorial use.
notes: The provider's erroneous 1865 metadata date does not change the public-domain status of the internally dated 1869 and 1886 printed components.
The cached PDF remains Git-ignored.

rights.component: All 415 Lambeth LUNA manuscript photographs, IIIF manifest metadata, and interface-associated reproductions
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The exact IIIF canvases identify rights only as 'LPL' and supply no open license. Lambeth's official forms restrict supplied copies to private study/research and require separate, use-specific permission for publication or other reproduction.
notes: Public viewing and private research caching do not authorize committing the bundle, redistributing it through the corpus repository, or using the images in profit-oriented print-on-demand products.
Lambeth's permission is non-exclusive, use-specific, non-transferable where a fee is paid, and must be renewed for revised editions or additional formats.

rights.component: DIMEV version 1.0 data files, documentation, and repository content
rights.basis: The release LICENSE, README, and Zenodo record apply Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International.

rights.component: Salisbury's 2002 edition, annotations, modern editorial text, and downloadable PDF
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: METS states that digital editions are for individual use only and directs users to the Executive Director for reprinting. Its CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 grant applies to XML, not automatically to the downloadable PDF or all editorial content.
notes: The medieval poem remains public domain; this record addresses the modern edition and PDF layer. The local PDF is a private research cache.

rights.component: Keller's 1842 volume and the complete Commons PDF scan
rights.jurisdiction: United States and source-country term represented by provider
rights.basis: Wikimedia Commons marks the exact file Public Domain and free of known restrictions, with Public Domain Mark 1.0; the underlying edition was published in 1842 and its editor died in 1883.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Scribal abbreviation marks and superscript or contracted letterforms in Lambeth MS 306
editorial_practices.representation: Expanded letters frequently printed in italic type; square brackets and footnotes mark some supplies, corrections, and uncertain readings
editorial_practices.description: Direct comparison of sampled manuscript leaves with the printed poem shows that Furnivall's italics often record expansion or editorial supply rather than semantic emphasis. The claim is limited to observed and encoded instances, not every italic occurrence without review.
editorial_practices.scope: Printed poem, especially sampled readings on ff. 178r-187r / printed pp. 1-19

editorial_practices.source_feature: The poem occupies one booklet and shares boundary leaves with medical recipes inside a 204-folio composite codex
editorial_practices.representation: A separately titled and paginated printed edition of the poem
editorial_practices.description: Furnivall extracted The Wright's Chaste Wife from MS 306, ff. 178r-187r; the edition does not reproduce the codex's adjacent recipes, other works, physical layout, or complete binding context.
editorial_practices.scope: Whole Furnivall edition relative to MS 306

editorial_practices.source_feature: A complete revised-edition volume with title page, preface, poem, notes, glossary, companion poem, and issue matter
editorial_practices.representation: The edition title page and complete poem only
editorial_practices.description: U-M encodes the revised-edition title page and printed pp. 1-19 / lines 1-672, retaining marginal and footnotes, but omits the preface, P.P.S. 1869, printed notes, glossary, companion poem 'Women,' and other matter.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Furnivall's typographic italics for many expanded or supplied letters
editorial_practices.representation: 110 HI elements with REND='italic' and 12 ABBR elements
editorial_practices.description: The XML retains much of Furnivall's italic distinction but is an encoding of editorial typography, not a representation of the manuscript's original abbreviation shapes. Twelve ABBR elements separately mark selected unexpanded forms.

evidence.citation: Reproducible element and attribute audit of General Corpus CME00054.xml.
evidence.summary: Counts 672 L, 6 LG, 202 NOTE (196 marginal and 6 foot), 20 PB, 242 MILESTONE (224 line-number and 18 manuscript-leaf milestones), 110 italic HI spans, and 12 ABBR elements.

evidence.citation: Visual and sequence audit of the complete Lambeth MS 306 IIIF bundle.

Also confirms sampled 1800-pixel color derivatives and native-size spine fallback.

evidence.summary: States that digital editions are for individual use only, directs reprint requests to the Executive Director, and applies CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 specifically to XML.

evidence.citation: Wikimedia Commons, complete PDF of Adelbert von Keller's Gesta Romanorum (1842), derived from IA dli.granth.37769.
evidence.locator: chapter 69 De castitate, printed pp. 107-109 / PDF pp. 115-117
evidence.summary: Complete 320-page scan confirming Keller's bibliographic facts and exact cited chapter. The Commons file page applies Public Domain Mark 1.0 and states the work is free of known copyright restrictions.

evidence.citation: Humboldt-Universität zu Berlin digital collections, Gesta Romanorum, BV046679278.
evidence.summary: Independent institutional complete-volume digital object confirming Keller's 1842 edition and persistent identifier urn:nbn:de:kobv:11-713535; its digitization is offered under CC BY 4.0.

open_questions.question: Is Keller's Gesta Romanorum chapter 69 a direct source of The Wright's Chaste Wife or one analogue within a wider tale tradition?
next_steps: Compare the English poem against multiple medieval witnesses of Gesta chapter 69 rather than only Keller's edited text.
Consult current source scholarship before adding a transcribes, version_of, or other direct derivation edge.
notes: The manifest records Furnivall's citation exactly while withholding a stronger derivation claim.

open_questions.question: Which readings and apparatus changed between Furnivall's 1865 first edition and the 1869 revised second edition, and did Furnivall recheck MS 306?
next_steps: Locate a verified scan of the 1865 first edition and compare it page by page with the cached 1869 revised second edition.
Consult EETS archives or correspondence for the revision method.

open_questions.question: Will Lambeth Palace Library permit redistribution of the complete or selected MS 306 images in the public General Corpus repository or their use in commercial and noncommercial print-on-demand publications?
next_steps: Submit the permission form naming the exact repository URL, complete 415-image private cache, proposed selection, print-on-demand formats, profit possibilities, and anticipated print runs.
Until written permission is received, keep all image bytes Git-ignored and publish only catalog, IIIF, folio, canvas, and request links.

open_questions.question: Can Lambeth provide a higher-resolution whole-manuscript delivery and missing top, fore, and bottom edge views while preserving the full physical-object sequence?
next_steps: Ask Lambeth whether preservation-master files already exist for all 415 published images and whether separate edge photographs can safely be made.
Request covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, endleaves, blanks, every folio side, adjacent matter, and available edges as one physical-object scope; give ff. 178r-187r only as the work mapping.
Discuss the order-form 40-scan limit before requesting a whole-codex replacement delivery.
notes: The current public bundle is complete for the 415-canvas provider sequence and already includes covers, pastedowns, all leaves, rear matter, and a separate spine image.
